source_old_req: The door device component must enforce deterministic boolean states for the actuator control commands swivel, de_arrest, and lock. It must guarantee that the physical feedback states handle_position, closed, latched, and locked satisfy the following progressive interlock logic: the latched state is only valid when the closed state is true, and the locked state is only valid when both the closed and latched states are true. Additionally, when the de_arrest command is true, the closed state must be false to prevent conflicts between the actuator and the mechanical structure. The system shall also ensure that the warning signals warn_diff_pres and warn_slide are correctly asserted based on the door's operational state.
natural_requirement: The doors_process component shall define the implementation-level signal routing for doors_process.imp. In the implementation layer, doors_process.imp shall route signal flow from D1_handle_position to door1.handle_position through connD11 and signal flow from D1_closed to door1.closed through connD12.

requirement_items.text: The doors_process.imp implementation shall route the signal flow from D1_handle_position to door1.handle_position through connection connD11.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The doors_process.imp implementation shall declare connection connD11 from D1_handle_position to door1.handle_position.

requirement_items.text: The doors_process.imp implementation shall route the signal flow from D1_closed to door1.closed through connection connD12.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The doors_process.imp implementation shall declare connection connD12 from D1_closed to door1.closed.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

natural_requirement_before_behavior_styl: The doors_process.imp implementation shall declare connection connD11 from D1_handle_position to door1.handle_position. The doors_process.imp implementation shall declare connection connD12 from D1_closed to door1.closed.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The doors_process.imp implementation shall route the signal flow from D1_handle_position to door1.handle_position through connection connD11. The doors_process.imp implementation shall route the signal flow from D1_closed to door1.closed through connection connD12.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
